mechanism_narrative: A sustained low federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, compressing equity risk premiums and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in low-rate periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate crossing above 9.0 percent signals severe labor market deterioration and collapsing consumer income, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns as earnings expectations are downgraded.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60 percent of triggered instances despite the negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a broad collapse in consumer demand, forcing corporate earnings revisions downward and producing negative forward equity returns.
failure_condition: The joint decline pattern fails to predict negative returns, with a win rate for positive returns exceeding 55 percent over a 3-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth exceeding 1.0 percent month-over-month signals strong supply-side momentum and capacity utilization, generating positive short-term S&P 500 returns through improved corporate margins.
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month growth fails to predict positive 5-day returns, with a directional accuracy below 50 percent in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A consumer price index level crossing above 225.0 triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which increases discount rates and compresses equity valuations, producing negative forward returns.
failure_condition: High inflation periods show positive 20-day returns in more than 60 percent of cases, contradicting the tightening expectation mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence where nonfarm payrolls rise while the unemployment rate falls indicates healthy labor absorption and wage growth, supporting positive equity returns through improved consumer spending forecasts.
failure_condition: The divergence pattern yields negative forward returns in more than 50 percent of observed cases over a rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product growth directly increases aggregate corporate earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns as equity valuations adjust to stronger economic fundamentals.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or GDP growth shows no statistically significant correlation with forward equity returns.

mechanism_narrative: In a liquidity trap regime, rising industrial production alongside stable retail sales signals demand-pull recovery and improved capacity utilization, driving positive forward equity returns.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict positive returns when the federal funds rate is below 0.25 percent, with a directional accuracy below 45 percent.
